mission: Transform black-box screenshot testing into white-box state queries by exposing structured UI element state, data sources, and linkage chains as first-class test primitives.

personas.name: Test Engineer
personas.description: QA or test automation engineer who writes and maintains end-to-end tests. Frustrated by flaky tests caused by timing issues, screenshot-based assertions that miss data correctness, and CSS selector fragility. Wants deterministic, fast, and semantically meaningful test assertions.
goals: Replace screenshot-based verification with structured state queries
Eliminate flaky tests caused by fixed timeouts and race conditions
Verify data linkage chains (filter change → API call → table update) in one assertion
Run the same test across multiple device profiles without rewriting
Get clear, actionable error messages when pre-checks fail (element not found, disabled, busy)
pain_points_addressed: Cannot verify data correctness from screenshots — only visual appearance (~80% accuracy)
Fixed setTimeout waits cause both slowness and flakiness
CSS selectors break on refactors without semantic meaning
No way to verify that a filter change actually triggered the right API call
Tests pass/fail inconsistently due to timing-dependent assertions
personas.interaction_pattern: Uses integration SDK (ProbePage for Playwright, ProbeTester for Flutter, ProbeXCTest for iOS, ProbeTestRule for Android, ProbeDevice for Detox). Writes test code against the typed probe API. Relies on waitForState/waitForPageReady for synchronization and actAndWait for composite operations. Uses custom matchers (toHaveProbeState, toHaveProbeData, toBeEffectivelyVisible) for readable assertions.
key_api_methods: query() — state assertion instead of screenshot
verifyLinkage() — explicit data flow chain validation
personas.success_metric: 60-page acceptance testing reduced from 2+ hours to 15-30 minutes; flaky tests eliminated by event-driven waits

personas.name: Frontend Developer
personas.description: Developer building the UI who adds probe annotations to key controls. Needs the annotation overhead to be minimal (5-10 annotations per page, <30 minutes) and zero-impact in production builds.
goals: Annotate key UI controls with minimal effort (<30 min per page for typical complexity)
Use platform-native idioms (HTML attributes, SwiftUI modifiers, Compose modifiers)
Ensure annotations have zero production impact (collector only injected at test time)
Express data linkages declaratively (data-probe-linkage JSON) for automatic verification
Get CLI support for auto-generating annotations on existing codebases
Validate annotation quality via CLI (no duplicate IDs, no orphan linkage targets)
pain_points_addressed: Test engineers constantly ask about element selectors and timing — probe gives them semantic access
Refactoring UI breaks tests written against implementation details
No standard way to express 'this dropdown controls that table' — linkage model solves this
data-testid attributes are arbitrary and don't carry semantic meaning
personas.interaction_pattern: Adds data-probe-* attributes (web), ProbeWidget wrappers (Flutter), .probeId() modifiers (SwiftUI/Compose), or attached properties (WPF/MAUI) to key UI controls. Uses CLI instrument command to auto-annotate existing projects. Uses validate command to check for duplicate IDs, orphan linkages, circular linkages. Uses report command to check coverage.

personas.name: SDK Contributor
personas.description: Engineer implementing a new platform SDK (e.g. adding Electron support, implementing a stub platform). Must understand the full UITestProbe interface, the ProbeElement model, and the 6-primitive contract. Works against spec/probe-types.ts as the authoritative contract.
goals: Implement the complete UITestProbe interface (6 primitives + hierarchy + platform context)
Map platform UI tree (DOM / Widget Tree / View Hierarchy / SemanticsNode) to ProbeElement model
Achieve conformance with spec/probe-types.ts type definitions
Match annotation style to platform idioms while preserving semantic equivalence
Integrate with the platform test framework (WidgetTester, XCTest, ComposeTestRule, etc.)
pain_points_addressed: No conformance test suite yet (spec/conformance-tests/ referenced but not present)
ProbeType enum divergence (7 vs 9 types) between platforms is undocumented at SDK level
personas.interaction_pattern: Reads spec/probe-types.ts as the contract. Reads sdk/web/ as the reference implementation. Implements the same interface in the target language. Writes annotations module (parse/attach metadata), collector module (registry + state observer + source tracker), and actions module (dispatcher with pre-checks). Wires together in a main class.

architecture_contract.annotations_module: Reads platform-native metadata and produces ProbeElement fields
architecture_contract.collector_module: Maintains live registry; observes state changes; intercepts network
architecture_contract.actions_module: Dispatches real platform events with 5-point pre-checks and linkage verification
architecture_contract.main_class: Composes all modules; implements UITestProbe interface

api_groups.name: Element Registry
api_groups.description: Register and query UI elements by semantic ID. The foundation primitive that all others depend on. Scans the UI tree (DOM, widget tree, view hierarchy) for annotated elements and maintains a live registry keyed by probe ID.

operations.description: Look up a single element by its semantic probe ID. Returns the full 15-attribute ProbeElement or null if not found.
Return all registered elements, optionally filtered by ProbeType (e.g. only 'data-container' or 'action' elements).
Return a summary of the current page: its ID, state, all elements, and which elements are still loading/submitting (unready). If no PAGE element found, derives state from unready elements count.

api_groups.name: State Exposure
api_groups.description: Expose real-time element state (loaded/error/disabled/submitting/etc.) as structured data instead of requiring screenshot interpretation. State includes current value, previous value, timestamp, open/closed for modals, and validation errors.

operations.description: Get the current state object for a specific element. Throws Error (not ProbeActionError) if element not found.
Batch query: get state objects for multiple elements at once. Silently skips IDs not found.

api_groups.name: Event Stream
api_groups.description: Subscribe to state changes and events using an event-driven model instead of polling or fixed timeouts. Provides promise-based waitFor methods for test synchronization and callback-based subscriptions for continuous monitoring.

operations.signature: (id: string, state: string, timeout?: number) => Promise<void>
operations.description: Wait until a specific element reaches the desired state string. Resolves immediately if already in that state. Rejects on timeout (default 10s).
Wait until the page is ready: either the PAGE element reaches 'loaded' state, or all elements leave loading/submitting states. Default timeout 30s.

operations.signature: (id: string, callback: (oldState: string, newState: string) => void) => () => void
operations.description: Register a callback that fires whenever the specified element's state changes. Returns an unsubscribe function.

operations.signature: (id: string, event: string, callback: (detail: unknown) => void) => () => void
operations.description: Subscribe to arbitrary probe events scoped to a specific element. Returns an unsubscribe function.

api_groups.name: Source Binding
api_groups.description: Link UI elements to their API data sources. Intercepts network requests and correlates them with elements annotated with data-probe-source. Tracks URL, method, HTTP status, response time, and payload.

operations.description: Get the source binding for an element: URL, method, status code, response time. Returns null if no source tracked.
Return the full network log since probe installation. Each entry includes URL, method, status, timestamp, and matched elementId if correlated.
Wait until a specific element's source binding is populated (the corresponding network request completes).

api_groups.name: Layout Metrics
api_groups.description: Provide position, size, visibility, scroll position, and render timing metrics from the rendering engine. Uses platform-native APIs (getBoundingClientRect + IntersectionObserver web, RenderBox flutter, UIView.frame iOS, SemanticsNode.boundsInWindow Android, UIElement.RenderSize Windows).

operations.description: Get current layout metrics: x, y, width, height, visible flag, render time, scroll offsets.

operations.signature: () => Array<{ a: string; b: string; overlapArea: number }>
operations.description: Detect all pairs of overlapping probe elements in the current viewport by computing bounding rect intersection areas.
Get the scroll position of a scrollable probe element in pixels.

api_groups.name: Action Dispatch
api_groups.description: Perform semantic UI operations using probe IDs with smart 5-point pre-checks (exists, visible, enabled, not busy, option exists for select). Every action dispatches real platform events. After each action, automatically verifies linkage targets if declared on the element.
pre_check_sequence: 1. Element exists in registry → NOT_FOUND if missing
4. state.current not in ['loading', 'submitting'] → BUSY if busy
5. For select only: value in data.options → OPTION_NOT_FOUND if missing

operations.description: Click an element with full 4-point pre-check (NOT_FOUND, NOT_VISIBLE, DISABLED, BUSY).
Double-click an element.
Right-click / context menu on an element.
Hover over element dispatching mouseenter and mouseover events.
Move keyboard focus to element.
Type text character-by-character dispatching keydown/keypress/input/keyup per character.
Set input value atomically (works with React controlled components via native setter + input/change events).
Clear an input element (implemented as fill(id, '')).
Select an option from a selector element. Validates option exists against data.options before dispatching (5th pre-check).
Set checked state of a checkbox or radio button.

operations.signature: (id: string, position: { top?: number; left?: number }) => Promise<void>
operations.description: Scroll element to absolute position.
Scroll element to maximum scroll position.
Scroll viewport to center element.
Drag-and-drop from source to target. Pre-checks BOTH source and target elements.
Press a keyboard shortcut (e.g. 'Ctrl+S'). No element pre-checks — dispatched to document/app.
Navigate to a route (history.pushState on web). No element pre-checks.
Capture a full snapshot of all registered elements with platform context. Used as regression baseline.
Compare two snapshots and return an array of field-level differences (elementId, field, before, after).
Verify all declared linkage targets responded correctly after an action. Returns structured result with directEffects, chainedEffects, and apiCalls.

operations.signature: (id: string, action: string, waitFor: { target?: string; state?: string; timeout?: number }) => Promise<ActAndWaitResult>
operations.description: Execute a semantic action then wait for a target element to reach a specific state. Automatically invokes verifyLinkage if linkage targets are declared. Returns actionDuration, waitDuration, targetState, and linkageResults.

api_groups.name: Hierarchy
api_groups.description: Navigate the parent-children tree of probe elements. Enables ancestor chain walking for effective visibility checks — child visible=true but parent hidden means effectively invisible.

operations.description: Get direct child elements by reading element.children[] probe IDs and resolving from registry.
Recursively collect all descendant probe elements in the subtree rooted at the given element.
Get the direct parent element by reading element.parent probe ID.
Walk the parent chain from root to the given element, returning all ancestors in order.
Check that every ancestor in the chain has layout.visible=true. Returns false if any ancestor is hidden.

api_groups.name: Platform Context
api_groups.description: Manage platform, device, and viewport context. Provides 11 built-in device presets and runAcrossDevices() for matrix testing across multiple device profiles with a single test function.

operations.description: Set the full platform context (platform, device profile, viewport size, input mode).
Get the current platform context. Web: auto-detects from navigator.userAgent + window.innerWidth if not explicitly set.

Apply a named device preset from spec/devices.json. Available: iphone-se, iphone-15-pro, ipad-air, ipad-pro-12, pixel-8, galaxy-s24, galaxy-tab-s9, galaxy-fold, macbook-air-13, desktop-1080p, desktop-1440p.

operations.signature: (devices: Array<ViewportPreset | string>, test: (probe: UITestProbe) => Promise<void>) => Promise<Record<string, TestResult>>
operations.description: Run the same test function across multiple device profiles sequentially. Returns { deviceName: { device, passed, duration, error? } }.

stages.name: Discover
activities: Reads README.md to understand the 6 primitives and two-layer architecture
Views the code examples showing query/waitForPageReady/actAndWait patterns
Understands the '7 testing pain points' table and maps to own testing frustrations
friction_points: README examples use page.probe shorthand but the actual Playwright integration requires ProbePage wrapper — the setup step is not shown
No quick-start tutorial scoped to a specific framework (React+Playwright, Flutter, etc.)

stages.name: Evaluate
activities: Reads DESIGN.md for full API surface and linkage model details
Reviews example project for own platform to see annotation patterns
Checks spec/probe-types.ts to understand the type contract
friction_points: DESIGN.md is written in Chinese — English developers need translation assistance
Example projects are not runnable yet (depend on SDK builds not published)

stages.name: Integrate
activities: Installs integration package (@allforai/ui-test-probe-playwright)
Builds sdk/web IIFE bundle (npm run build in sdk/web/) as prerequisite
Adds injectProbe() to test setup / playwright.config.ts global fixtures
Wraps Playwright Page in ProbePage for typed probe access
Registers custom matchers via expect.extend()
friction_points: Must build sdk/web IIFE bundle first — dist/probe-bundle.js dependency is not pre-published
ProbePage is not automatically injected — requires explicit new ProbePage(page) in each test

stages.name: Use
activities: Writes tests using probe.query() for state assertions instead of screenshots
Replaces page.waitForTimeout() with probe.waitForPageReady()
Uses probe.actAndWait() for interaction + synchronization in one call
Uses probe.verifyLinkage() to validate data flow chains
Uses probe.runAcrossDevices() for responsive testing across device presets
friction_points: Requires frontend developer to have added annotations before query() returns data
actAndWait() action string parsing ('select:completed') is convention-based — easy to misformat

stages.name: Extend
activities: Creates domain-specific test utilities composing probe methods
Builds custom Playwright matchers beyond the 3 built-in ones
Uses snapshot/diff for regression testing baselines
friction_points: No documented plugin or extension API

stages.name: Discover
activities: Learns about probe annotation from test engineer or README
Understands the annotation cost table from DESIGN.md section 5
Confirms that collector is only injected at test time — zero production impact
friction_points: DESIGN.md is in Chinese — no standalone English annotation guide exists

stages.name: Integrate
activities: Runs CLI instrument command to auto-annotate an existing codebase
Manually adds/refines probe annotations to key controls (5-10 per page)
Declares linkage targets for elements that drive downstream elements
Runs CLI validate to check for annotation errors
friction_points: Linkage path type selection (direct vs api vs store) requires understanding the data flow architecture
No IDE plugin for attribute autocomplete or inline validation

stages.name: Use
activities: Runs CLI validate to check for duplicate IDs, orphan linkages, circular linkages
Runs CLI report to see per-page and per-type annotation coverage
Keeps annotations updated as components evolve
friction_points: No warning when refactoring renames IDs that linkage targets reference

stages.name: Discover
activities: Reads README.md and DESIGN.md section 4 (cross-platform SDK architecture diagram)
Identifies the 3-layer pattern: annotations → collector → actions
friction_points: No SDK porting guide or CONTRIBUTING.md
ProbeType enum divergence (7 vs 9 types in existing platforms) is undocumented

stages.name: Integrate
activities: Ports ProbeElement model to target language
Implements AnnotationParser equivalent reading platform-native metadata
Implements ElementRegistry using platform tree scanning APIs
Implements ActionDispatcher with 5-point pre-check pattern
friction_points: Pre-check pattern must be faithfully reproduced — error codes must match spec
Source tracking (network interception) is complex and varies significantly per platform

design_principles.name: Annotation-Based, Not Intrusive
design_principles.description: The framework requires only declarative annotations on UI elements — no business logic coupling, no test framework code in production components. The runtime collector is injected exclusively at test time.
design_principles.impact: Frontend developers add 5-10 attribute annotations per page without changing component logic. Production builds exclude the collector entirely.

design_principles.name: Event-Driven Waits, Not Polling
design_principles.description: All synchronization uses event notifications or MutationObserver, not setTimeout loops. waitForState() resolves the instant the state changes, not after a fixed delay.
design_principles.impact: Tests run as fast as the application, not on worst-case timing. A page that loads in 200ms resolves waitForPageReady() in ~200ms, not after a 2-second sleep.

design_principles.name: Smart Pre-Checks on Every Action
design_principles.description: Every action method validates element state through a 5-point check sequence before dispatching. Failures throw structured ProbeActionError objects with machine-readable error codes.
design_principles.impact: Actions never silently fail. A click on a disabled button throws DISABLED with the validation error reason.

design_principles.name: Automatic Linkage Verification
design_principles.description: When an element has linkage declarations, actions automatically verify that all downstream targets responded correctly — without any extra test code.
design_principles.impact: A single actAndWait() call tests the full data flow chain: action → state transition → linked targets → API calls.

design_principles.name: Semantic Identity, Not Selector Fragility
design_principles.description: Elements are identified by stable semantic IDs (e.g. 'order-list', 'status-filter') rather than CSS selectors, XPath, or DOM structure.
design_principles.impact: UI refactors that rename CSS classes or restructure DOM hierarchy do not break tests.

design_principles.name: Progressive Disclosure
design_principles.description: The minimum viable annotation is a single data-probe-id attribute. Additional attributes (type, state, source, linkage) add capability progressively without requiring upfront investment in the full model.
design_principles.impact: A developer can add data-probe-id to one element and immediately have query() returning data.

design_principles.name: Platform Abstraction via Uniform Model
design_principles.description: All platforms map to the same ProbeElement model (15 attributes, 9 types) defined in spec/probe-types.ts. Test logic written against one platform's probe API is conceptually transferable to all others.
design_principles.impact: A test engineer writing against the probe API can transfer skills across platforms.

design_principles.name: Convention Over Configuration
design_principles.description: Sensible defaults, shorthand formats, and auto-detection reduce explicit configuration. The source shorthand 'GET /api/orders' is easier than full JSON; device auto-detection works without any setup.
design_principles.impact: New annotations require only data-probe-id to function. Source bindings work with a simple URL string.

design_principles.name: Hierarchy-Aware Visibility
design_principles.description: Visibility is not just a per-element flag but an ancestor-chain property. isEffectivelyVisible() walks the full parent chain, so a child with visible=true inside a hidden container correctly reports as not visible.
design_principles.impact: Visibility-toggle linkage effects are correctly verified without false positives.

implementation_roadmap: Phase 1: Web SDK (TypeScript) + Playwright integration — reference implementation with all 6 primitives. Examples: web-react, web-vue.
Phase 2: Flutter SDK (Dart) + flutter_test integration — ProbeWidget, ProbeBinding, HttpOverrides. Example: flutter.
Phase 3: iOS SDK (Swift/SwiftUI) + XCTest integration; Android SDK (Kotlin/Compose) + Compose UI Test integration. Examples: ios-swiftui, android-compose.
Phase 4: Windows SDKs (WPF/WinForms/WinUI, C#) + test drivers; React Native SDK (TypeScript) + Detox integration; CLI tool (instrument/validate/report). Examples: windows-maui, react-native.
Phase 5: All example projects runnable with proper dependencies, cross-platform conformance test suite, CONTRIBUTING.md, English translations of DESIGN.md and SPEC.md.

documentation_state.primary_gap: No getting-started tutorial exists anywhere. A new user cannot follow a step-by-step guide from zero to a working test.
documentation_state.language_accessibility_issue: SPEC.md and DESIGN.md are written in Chinese (Mandarin). This limits accessibility for non-Chinese-speaking contributors.

documents.critical_issue: Code examples use 'page.probe.query()' shorthand that does not match the actual Playwright integration API (ProbePage wrapper).
Not accessible to English-speaking contributors without translation.
Most complete document but inaccessible to non-Chinese readers. spec/conformance-tests/ referenced but does not exist.

top_3_recommendations: Write a 15-minute getting started tutorial against examples/web-react/ — highest adoption impact.
Translate DESIGN.md to English or write an equivalent English technical reference.
Fix the README code examples to match the actual Playwright ProbePage API.
